front: Opera: French opera by Léo Delibes set in British India; Brahmin priestess falls in love with British officer Gérald; she eats a poisonous datura leaf when he must return to military duty
Opera: the priest Nilakantha forces his daughter to sing the Bell Song in public to lure and identify the British soldier who trespassed on sacred Brahmin grounds
Opera: the opera fell into obscurity until a British Airways commercial in the 1980s used the Flower Duet and revived interest in it
Opera: premiered at the Opéra-Comique, which also hosted the premieres of Carmen and Pelléas et Mélisande

Opera: Act I duet in B major, 6/8 time, with extended parallel-third melismas; sung by Lakmé and her servant Mallika as they gather flowers by a river; opening melody is D-sharp – C-sharp – D-sharp – E
Opera: its Flower Duet describes a 'thick dome' and 'dense canopy' of jasmine and opens with lyrics about 'jasmine white' under a 'leafy dome'
Opera: coloratura showpiece in which the title character narrates how a young girl saved a stranger from wild animals by charming them with bells; the glockenspiel echoes her voice
